name: Woodsy Mushroom Goulash
recipeIngredient: 4 pale green bell peppers, cored, seeded, and cut crosswise into 1/4-inch pieces
1 1/4 pounds large white mushrooms, stems trimmed, and quartered
3 cups cooked egg noodles or rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a pot of water to a boil and prepare the tomatoes.
- Cut an "X" into each tomato's bottom.
- Place in boiling water for about 30 seconds to loosen skins.
- Remove and cool, then peel and chop.
2. Heat oil over low heat in a large pot. Add onions and cook, stirring until wilted.
3. Sprinkle paprika over the onions and cook for one minute.
4. Add peppers and reserved tomatoes; cook on medium heat for 10 minutes, stirring once or twice.
5. Add mushrooms to the vegetables and stir well.
6. Cook goulash for an additional 5 minutes.
7. In a small bowl, whisk together yogurt, sour cream, and flour.
- Gradually add half of the vegetable juices.
8. Whisk in this mixture into the mushroom-vegetable goulash.
9. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Before serving, gently reheat the goulash on low heat, stirring. Avoid boiling.
11. Serve immediately over noodles or rice and garnish with chopped parsley.
Serves 6. Per serving: 251 calories; 8 grams fat; 26 milligrams cholesterol.
